info.name: Courier Mobile API
info.description:
API endpoints for courier mobile app (v2.9) — All routes use authenticated courier from Sanctum token (no courier ID needed in URL).

v2.9 Changes (April 2026):
- RENAMED: `rts_count` → `failed_delivery_count` in all delivery responses (list, detail, PATCH)
- RENAMED: `rts_attempts` → `failed_delivery_attempts` in delivery detail and PATCH responses
- FIXED: `rts_verification_status` default value is now lowercase `unvalidated` (was `UNVALIDATED`)
- NOTE: `rts_verification_status` key name is retained for backward compatibility

v2.8 Changes (April 2026):
- FIXED: GET /api/mbl/deliveries?status=FAILED_DELIVERY now returns correct results (FAILED_DELIVERY alias was not being normalized to RTS before the DB query — returned 0 rows).
- RENAMED: GET /api/mbl/dashboard-summary response key `rts` is now `failed_delivery` to match the mobile status contract. Update any read of `data.rts` to `data.failed_delivery`.
- STATUS CONTRACT FINAL: The mobile app must never send or display the term RTS. The canonical mobile status is FAILED_DELIVERY. RTS is an internal DB/web-only term.

v2.7 Changes (April 2026):
- UPDATED: Mobile status alias contract — internal RTS is now returned to mobile clients as FAILED_DELIVERY. Incoming FAILED_DELIVERY is normalized to RTS server-side for backward-compatible persistence.
- UPDATED: POST /api/mbl/check-dispatch-eligibility now supports device-only prechecks (without partial_code) and enforces configurable free-storage threshold via mobile.dispatch_eligibility.min_free_storage_gb.
- UPDATED: PATCH /api/mbl/deliveries/{barcode} returns machine-readable error code DELIVERY_IMMUTABLE and HTTP 409 for immutable delivered records.
- UPDATED: PATCH /api/mbl/deliveries/{barcode} is throttled (60/min) so Laravel includes Retry-After on 429 responses.
- UPDATED: POST /api/mbl/deliveries/{barcode}/media accepts media type other; supported types are pod, selfie, recipient_signature, other.

v2.6 Changes (April 2026):
- SIMPLIFIED: dispatch_code now always equals partial_code across all dispatch endpoints and delivery items. The separate partial_code field has been removed from all mobile API responses — there is only one code the courier ever needs: dispatch_code.
- UPDATED: GET /api/mbl/notifications — new_dispatch type: partial_code field removed; dispatch_code is now the partial batch code.

v2.5 Changes (April 2026):
- ADDED: POST /api/mbl/profile/fcm-token — Update FCM Token (Register / refresh device token)
- DOCUMENTED: FCM Token Resilience Contract — backend auto-clears UNREGISTERED tokens; mobile must sync on every startup and onTokenRefresh

v2.4 Changes (April 2026):
- NEW: Notification types expanded — GET /api/mbl/notifications now returns two additional types:
  • new_dispatch — Fired when a DOP batch is dispatched to the courier. Extra fields: dispatch_code (partial batch code), delivery_count, action ("new_dispatch").
  • payout_paid  — Fired when HR marks a payout request as paid. Fields: transaction_reference, delivery_references, amount, date.
- UPDATED: GET /api/mbl/notifications response shape adds nullable fields on all items: stage, rejection_reason, dispatch_code, delivery_count, action. Null for types that do not use them.
- All push notifications (FCM) are now recorded to the notifications table so they are readable/unreadable via the Notifications API.

v2.3 Changes (April 2026):
- NEW: GET /api/mbl/app/version — Returns { min_version, force_update }. No authentication required.
- NEW: POST /api/mbl/courier/reports — Submit bug reports with device info and severity. Requires authentication.

v2.2 Changes (April 2026):
- NEW: GET /api/mbl/me/payment-method — Returns the courier's active bank account for payouts. Includes auto-provisioning message when bank is missing or inactive.
- NEW: Dynamic payout validation — Any active bank in the system is now accepted (removed GCash/BDO-only restriction).
- NEW: Auto-provisioning fallback — If a courier has no active bank on file when submitting a payout, a default GCash (00000000) account is automatically created.

v2.1 Changes (April 2026):
- NEW: GET /api/mbl/me — Courier profile endpoint (name, branch, phone, is_active).
- NEW: PATCH /api/mbl/me — Update profile information (username, email, names).
- NEW: POST /api/mbl/me/media — Profile picture upload (fallback for non-S3-direct clients).
- NEW: delivered_date field on PATCH deliveries — honors device timestamp for offline sync. Falls back to NOW() when absent.
- NEW: X-Request-ID idempotency header on PATCH deliveries — prevents duplicate processing on retry.
- NEW: transaction_at echoed in PATCH response so mobile can confirm what was stored.
- NEW: updated_since query param on GET deliveries — delta sync (1 call instead of 4).
- NEW: updated_at field on every GET deliveries list item — use as next updated_since value.

v2.0 Changes (March 2026):
- NEW: is_paid boolean on each delivery item.
- NEW: paid query param on deliveries and dashboard-summary endpoints.
- NEW: Slim List View vs Rich Detail View for deliveries.
- UPDATED: Payout API response cleanup (removed redundant timestamps, added history snapshot).
- UPDATED: Image fields (media, signature, rts attempt images) removed from all GET/PATCH delivery responses. Images are read-only on the web portal via signed URLs.

request.description: Login courier using phone number and password. Returns Sanctum token. Required fields: phone_number, password. Optional fields: device_name, device_identifier, device_type (android, ios, flutter, web), app_version.

response.name: 200 - Login Success
401 - Invalid Credentials

request.description: Reset courier password using courier code (no auth required)

response.name: 200 - Password Reset
404 - Invalid Courier Code

request.description: Logout courier and revoke all Sanctum tokens

response.name: 200 - Logout

request.description: Revoke all Sanctum tokens across all devices for the authenticated courier

response.name: 200 - All Sessions Revoked

request.description: Welcoming endpoint for the Courier Mobile API. Returns a simple JSON with success and a welcome message. Use this for connection/health checks. Requires Sanctum authentication.

response.name: 200 - Health OK

request.description: Change the authenticated courier's password. Requires current_password to verify identity.

response.name: 200 - Password Changed
422 - Invalid Current Password

request.description: Get partial dispatches pending acceptance for authenticated courier (status: DISPATCHED). Each item represents one partial batch the courier can individually accept or reject. Returns dispatch_code (partial batch code), status, courier_code, volume, item_count (actual delivery count), branch (branch_name, branch_code), tat, and transmittal_date. Paginated, default 10 per page.

response.name: 200 - Pending Dispatches

request.description: Accept a dispatch for authenticated courier (courier ID automatically used from token). dispatch_code must be uppercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores. client_request_id must be a valid UUID. Only this specific dispatch batch is accepted and moved to COURIER_RECEIVED. device_info.os must be one of: android, ios, windows, macos, linux.

response.name: 200 - Dispatch Accepted
response.body:

response.name: 400 - Not Authorized

request.description: Reject a specific dispatch by authenticated courier with a mandatory reason. dispatch_code must be uppercase alphanumeric. client_request_id must be a valid UUID. Only batches with status 'DISPATCHED' can be rejected. Batch stays in 'DISPATCHED' status but rejection is logged in history.

response.name: 200 - Dispatch Rejected
400 - Invalid Status

request.description: Check dispatch eligibility for the authenticated courier. Supports two modes: (1) device-only precheck without partial_code, and (2) full dispatch eligibility with partial_code. Device telemetry fields (free_storage_gb, os_version, app_version, device_type) are accepted for server-side validation and audit context.

response.name: 200 - Dispatch Eligible
response.body:
{
    "success": true,
    "message": "Operation completed successfully",

        "os_version": "14",
        "app_version": "1.2.3",
        "device_type": "android"
    }
}

query.description: PENDING, DELIVERED, FAILED_DELIVERY, OSA. Use FAILED_DELIVERY for failed delivery attempts (maps to internal RTS). Never send RTS from the mobile app. Omit when using updated_since to get all statuses.
true or false. Ignored when updated_since is provided.
NEW v2.0 — true | false | all (default: all). Filter by payment status.

NEW v2.1 — ISO 8601 UTC. Returns only deliveries whose server updated_at is after this timestamp. Use for delta sync on reconnect. Disable the status filter when using this param to receive all statuses in one call.

request.description:
Get deliveries for authenticated courier (no courier ID needed). Default per_page is 10.

### v2.1 — Delta Sync: `updated_since`
Send the ISO 8601 timestamp of the last successful sync. The server returns only records whose `updated_at` is after that timestamp, regardless of status. This reduces a typical reconnect from 4 status-filtered calls to **1 call**.

**Recommended mobile flow:**
```
On reconnect:
  GET /deliveries?updated_since={lastSyncTimestamp}&per_page=100
  → process each item by its delivery_status field
  → save new lastSyncTimestamp = max(updated_at) from response

First-time / full sync (no saved timestamp):
  Fall back to 4 separate status calls (existing behavior)
```

Each item now includes `updated_at` (server timestamp). Use it as the next `updated_since` value.

### v2.0 Changes:
- NEW: is_paid boolean on each delivery item (true = remittance collected).
- NEW: paid query param (true | false | all, default all) to filter by payment status.
- OPTIMIZED: Slim Payload — heavy fields (history, media, signature, etc.) are removed from the list view to improve performance. Use the Get Delivery Details endpoint (by barcode) for rich data.

request.description:
Get specific delivery details by barcode value. Returns full delivery record including failed delivery attempt metadata and transaction history. Image data (media, signature, rts attempt images) is not included in the mobile response — images are accessible via the web portal only (signed URLs). failed_delivery_count indicates the number of FAILED_DELIVERY attempts; failed_delivery_attempts contains attempt metadata (reason, geolocation, timestamp) without image URLs. These fields retain their legacy names for backward compatibility — semantically they represent failed delivery attempts, not "Return to Sender".

v2.0 Changes:
- NEW: is_paid boolean (true = remittance collected).
- REMOVED: tat_info object — the tat field already provides the TAT due datetime.
- REMOVED: media, signature fields — images are read-only on the web portal.
- UPDATED: rts_attempts no longer includes images array.

response.name: 404 - Delivery Not Found

formdata.description: Image file (jpeg, jpg, png) or PDF. Max 10MB.
Must be one of: pod, selfie, recipient_signature, other

request.description:
Upload media for a delivery by barcode value. This endpoint handles the actual file upload to S3 and returns the URLs (including signed URLs) for the uploaded media. These URLs should then be passed to the 'Update Delivery Status' endpoint in the 'delivery_images' or 'recipient_signature' fields.

### Required Multipart Fields:
- **file**: Image (jpeg, png, jpg) or PDF, max 10MB.
- **type**: Must be one of: pod, selfie, recipient_signature, other.

Note: The upload endpoint stores the file in S3 and returns the raw URL for the courier app's internal reference only. Image URLs are not echoed back in subsequent GET/PATCH delivery responses — image viewing is handled exclusively by the web portal using signed URLs.

response.name: 200 - Media Uploaded
404 - Not Found

header.description: v2.1 — UUID per sync operation. Server caches the response for 24h and returns it immediately on replay (idempotent retry). Generate a fresh UUID per delivery action.

request.description:
Update delivery status by barcode value. delivery_status must be one of: PENDING, DELIVERED, FAILED_DELIVERY, RTS, OSA. FAILED_DELIVERY is the mobile alias that maps to RTS in storage.

### v2.1 — Offline Sync: `delivered_date`
- **Optional ISO 8601 UTC string.** Send the device timestamp captured at the moment the rider tapped Delivered/Failed Delivery/OSA.
- The server uses this as the authoritative `transaction_at` and `delivered_date` instead of `NOW()`, so offline rides sync the real action time, not the upload time.
- Applies to all terminal statuses: DELIVERED, FAILED_DELIVERY (stored as RTS), RTS, OSA.
- **Validation**: rejected only if unparseable or more than 24 hours in the future. Past dates (hours or days ago) are accepted.

### v2.1 — Idempotency: `X-Request-ID`
- Send a fresh UUID in the `X-Request-ID` header for each delivery action.
- If the same UUID arrives again (retry/replay), the server returns the previously stored 200 result without reprocessing.
- Cache window: 24 hours.

### Conditional Fields:
- **DELIVERED**: Requires `recipient` (string). `relationship` and `placement_type` are highly recommended.
- **FAILED_DELIVERY / OSA**: Requires `reason` (string) for tracking. FAILED_DELIVERY also creates a new attempt record and increments `failed_delivery_count`.

### Media Handling:
- `delivery_images` and `recipient_signature` can accept BOTH file uploads (as multipart/form-data) or pre-uploaded URL strings (as JSON raw).
- URL strings are preferred for mobile apps that already uploaded to S3 via the /media endpoint.
- Note: After a successful status update, the response no longer includes media, recipient_signature, or image data in failed_delivery_attempts. These are accessible via the web portal only.

### Geolocation:
Optional fields (latitude, longitude, geo_accuracy) capture the courier's GPS coordinates at the time of the update.

### Delivery Confirmation Code:
- **Optional alphanumeric string (max 50 characters).** A confirmation code provided by the recipient at the time of delivery.
- Only relevant when `delivery_status` is `DELIVERED`.
- Accepts letters and numbers only (no spaces or special characters).

            }
        ],
        "delivery_confirmation_code": null
    }
}

response.name: 409 - Immutable Status
400 - Invalid Transition
422 - Invalid delivered_date

request.description:
Preview the upcoming payment request for the authenticated courier. Call this GET before the POST to let the courier review what will be included.

### Consolidation Logic:
1. **Cumulative Selection**: The system automatically bundles ALL successful deliveries (`delivered` or `rts` with pay) from the earliest unpaid date up to today.
2. **Exclusion Rule**: Deliveries already associated with an existing (pending/approved/paid) payment request are strictly excluded.
3. **One Request Per Day**: Couriers are restricted to one payout request submission per day. If a request was already submitted today, `has_existing_request_today` will be `true` and the POST endpoint will be blocked.

### Response Fields:
- `coverage_period`: { `from_date`, `to_date` } — Derived from actual earliest and latest eligible deliveries.
- `eligible_delivery_count`: Number of new items to be included.
- `already_requested_count`: Items excluded because they are already in another request.
- `estimated_net_payable`: The final amount the courier expects to receive after all deductions.
- `daily_breakdown`: ASC sorted list of days, each containing a full delivery sub-list with rates and penalties.

response.name: 200 - Payment Preview
response.body:
{
    "success": true,
    "data": {
        "coverage_period": {
            "from_date": "2026-02-10",
            "to_date": "2026-03-10"
        },
        "eligible_delivery_count": 25,
        "already_requested_count": 5,
        "has_existing_request_today": false,
        "estimated_gross_amount": 5000,
        "estimated_total_penalties": 250,
        "estimated_coordinator_incentive": 500,
        "estimated_net_payable": 4250,
        "daily_breakdown": [
            {
                "date": "2026-03-10",
                "delivery_count": 2,
                "day_total": 150,
                "deliveries": [
                    {
                        "barcode": "FSIEE586361",
                        "name": "ROMEO CRIZALDO LANUZA",
                        "address": "BLK 10 LOT 5 GRANDSTRIKEVILLE 4",
                        "delivery_status": "DELIVERED",
                        "rate_type": "express",
                        "rate": 200,
                        "penalty_amount": 50,
                        "coordinator_incentive": 20,
                        "net_amount": 130
                    },
                    {
                        "barcode": "HSKFIWHUFF",
                        "name": "JOEMAR JANE",
                        "address": "CABUYAO LAGUNA",
                        "delivery_status": "FAILED_DELIVERY",
                        "rate_type": "traditional",
                        "rate": 10,
                        "penalty_amount": 0,
                        "coordinator_incentive": 0,
                        "net_amount": 10
                    }
                ]
            }
        ]
    }
}

request.description:
Create a new payment request for the authenticated courier. This consolidates all eligible unpaid deliveries into a single 'pending' request.

### Business Rules:
- **Dates are Optional**: If `from_date` or `to_date` are omitted, the system defaults to 'ALL previous unpaid deliveries' (cumulative).
- **Submission Limit**: Restricted to **one** submission per day. Attempting a second request will return a `400 Bad Request`.
- **Status Lifecycle**: New requests start as `pending`. They move to `ops_approved` after Operations review, then to `hr_approved` and finally `paid`.

### Request Body:
- `from_date` (optional): Start of range (Y-m-d).
- `to_date` (optional): End of range (Y-m-d).

response.name: 200 - Payment Request Created
response.body:
{
    "success": true,
    "message": "Payment request created successfully",
    "data": {
        "payment_request_id": 1001,
        "reference": "PR-2026-001001",
        "courier_id": 543,
        "from_date": "2026-02-10",
        "to_date": "2026-03-10",
        "amount": 4250,
        "delivery_count": 25,
        "status": "PENDING"
    }
}

query.description: NEW v2.0 — true | false | all (default: all). Filters delivered_today count by payment status.

request.description:
Get dashboard summary for authenticated courier. Returns: pending_dispatches, delivered_today, failed_delivery count (internal RTS status), osa count, and pending_deliveries (accepted but not yet delivered).

v2.8 Changes:
- RENAMED: `rts` key is now `failed_delivery` — update any `data.rts` read to `data.failed_delivery`.

v2.0 Changes:
- NEW: paid query param (true | false | all, default all) — filters the delivered_today counter by payment status.
- delivered_today with paid=false returns only unpaid deliveries completed today.
- delivered_today with paid=all (default) returns all deliveries completed today regardless of payment status.

response.name: 200 - Dashboard (all)
200 - Dashboard (paid=false)

request.description:
Get a high-level wallet summary for the authenticated courier.

### Key Metrics:
- `total_earnings`: Cumulative sum of all requests with status `paid`.
- `tentative_pending_payout`: Sum of all requests currently in `pending`, `ops_approved`, or `hr_approved` status. This represents 'money on the way'.
- `latest_request`: Full details and earnings breakdown for the courier's most recent submission.

response.name: 200 - Wallet Summary
500 - Wallet Summary Error
200 - Wallet Detail
404 - Not Found
500 - Wallet Detail Error

request.description:
NEW v2.1 — Returns the full profile of the authenticated courier.

Use this endpoint when the Profile screen opens (online only) to refresh the locally cached courier object. The result should overwrite the courier data stored in secure storage so subsequent cold-starts have up-to-date name, branch, and profile photo without another API call.

- No request body or path param needed — identity comes from the Sanctum token.
- `profile_picture_url` is a signed S3 URL or null; show an avatar placeholder when null.
- `branch.branch_code` is now included (was missing from earlier responses).

response.name: 200 - Courier Profile
404 - No Courier Account

request.description:
Update the authenticated courier's profile information. Maps name/first_name/middle_name/last_name/email/profile_picture_url to the users table.

- `name` is required and must be unique (username).
- `email` is optional and must be unique.
- `profile_picture_url` is optional. If supplied, it overwrites the stored profile_picture column (useful for mobile apps using direct S3 upload).

response.name: 200 - Profile Updated

formdata.description: Image file (jpeg, png, jpg, webp). Max 10MB.

request.description:
Upload a profile picture for the authenticated courier. Handles the file upload to S3 and persists the URL to the user's profile.

### Required Multipart Fields:
- **file**: Image (jpeg, png, jpg, webp), max 10MB.
- **type**: Must be 'profile_picture'.

response.name: 200 - Profile Media Uploaded

request.description:
Register or refresh the FCM device token for the authenticated courier.

**When to call this endpoint:**
- On every app startup (even if the courier was previously logged in)
- From `FirebaseMessaging.instance.onTokenRefresh` whenever Firebase rotates the token
- After a fresh login (covered by `PushNotificationService.init()` being idempotent)

**Token Resilience Contract:**

The backend stores the token in `users.fcm_token`. When a push notification fires and Firebase returns `UNREGISTERED` (app reinstalled, token invalidated), the backend automatically clears this field so no further FCM sends are attempted for that stale token. Notifications are **always** recorded to the `notifications` table regardless of FCM outcome — the courier will see them on next app open.

**This endpoint must succeed silently.** A failed token sync must never block login or any delivery workflow; log the error and continue.

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fcm_token` | string | ✅ | Raw Firebase device token. Max 4 096 chars. |
| `device_type` | string | optional | `android`, `ios`, or `web`. Used for diagnostics only. |

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
NEW v2.2 — Returns the authenticated courier's active bank account information for payouts.

- `has_active_payment_method: true` — Courier has an active bank on file. Use the returned details to display their payout account in the app.
- `has_active_payment_method: false` — No active bank found. The `message` field explains that a default GCash (00000000) account will be **automatically set up** the moment the courier submits a payout request. No manual action from the courier is required.

### When to call this
- On the Payout / Wallet screen to show the courier which bank their payment will be sent to.
- Before showing the 'Request Payout' button to pre-warn users who need a bank update (though the system will auto-provision GCash as a fallback).

response.name: 200 - Active Bank (e.g. EWB)
200 - No Active Bank (Auto-Fallback Info)
200 - No Bank on File (Auto-Fallback Info)
404 - No Courier Account

request.description: Send a background location ping for authenticated courier. Used to power real-time ops mapping and calculate online presence.

response.name: 200 - Location Received

request.description:
Get paginated notifications for authenticated courier (most recent first).

### Notification Types

| type | Trigger | Extra fields |
|---|---|---|
| `payout_requested` | Courier submits a payout request | transaction_reference, delivery_references, amount |
| `payout_approved` | OPS or HR approves payout | transaction_reference, delivery_references, amount, stage (ops \| hr) |
| `payout_rejected` | OPS or HR rejects payout | transaction_reference, delivery_references, amount, stage, rejection_reason |
| `payout_paid` | HR marks payout as paid | transaction_reference, delivery_references, amount |
| `new_dispatch` | DOP batch dispatched to courier | dispatch_code (= partial_code), delivery_count, action ("new_dispatch") |
| `transaction_due_today` | Deliveries due today | delivery_references |
| `transaction_due_soon` | Deliveries due soon | delivery_references |

### Common fields (all types)
- `id`: UUID (use this for mark-as-read)
- `message`: human-readable string
- `date`: ISO 8601 timestamp
- `read`: boolean
- `read_at`: ISO 8601 timestamp or null

### Type-specific fields (null when not applicable)
- `transaction_reference`: payment request reference (e.g. PR-2026-001) or null
- `delivery_references[]`: array of barcode strings or empty array
- `amount`: float or null
- `stage`: "ops" | "hr" | null (payout_approved / payout_rejected only)
- `rejection_reason`: string or null (payout_rejected only)
- `dispatch_code`: string or null (new_dispatch only)
- `delivery_count`: int or null (new_dispatch only)
- `action`: string or null (new_dispatch only — value: "new_dispatch")

Also returns `unread_count` and `meta` (pagination).

response.name: 200 - Notifications
response.body:
{

request.description:
Submit a bug report from the courier mobile app. Requires authentication.

The payload expects flat device-info fields and a structured logs array. `type` must be one of: bug, enhancement, task, feedback. The response returns HTTP 201 with the generated `report_id` in RPT-xxxx format.

response.name: 201 - Report Submitted
